title: Sweet & Chewy Syrian Wood Ear Mushrooms Stir-fry
description: This dish features sweet and chewy Syrian wood ear mushrooms in a delightful pickle-based sauce, perfect for adventurous palates seeking something both sweet and savory with diabetic-friendly options.
instructions: Clean and soak the wood ear mushrooms in water to remove excess spongy texture, then slice them thinly.
Slice the garlic finely and chop the chili peppers if using. Soak pickled vegetables in cold water for 10 minutes before use to soften them slightly.
In a wok or skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add sliced wood ear mushrooms and sauté until they start to crisp up and release moisture, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the garlic, chili peppers (if using), and pickled vegetables. Stir well for about 2-3 more minutes.
Pour in soy sauce and mirin or rice wine vinegar. Adjust seasoning with sugar to taste. Cook until thickened, around 2-3 more minutes.
Serve hot as an appetizer or snack with steamed jasmine rice on the side.
